notes: This file is owned by Agent 09 only.
Structure nests rounds inside sprints to keep history organized.

sprint_meta.notes: WK30 R1: Agent 09 consult — silhouette + palette cohesion vs wk25/wk26; Agents 15/03/12 implement prefabs + default-on loader + validator.

what_i_changed.summary: Consult only (PM): no prefab or code edits. Confirmed Agents 15+03 landed: required prefabs under assets/prefabs/buildings/ (castle_v1, warrior_guild_v1, ranger_guild_v1, rogue_guild_v1, peasant_house_small_v1) plus optional wizard_guild_v1; piece counts castle=20 (at budget), warrior=8, ranger=6, rogue=6, wizard=11, house=2. game/graphics/ursina_renderer.py uses _resolve_prefab_path (default-on; KINGDOM_URSINA_PREFAB_TEST=0 opt-out), _PREFAB_BUILDING_TYPE_TO_FILE house→peasant_house_small_v1.json, convention <type>_v1.json otherwise; _load_prefab_instance auto-centers XZ per WK30 hotfix notes. Style (data review): Castle mixes Retro Fantasy + Survival Kit — good visual anchor vs green terrain; guild set stays Retro Fantasy — cohesive low-poly fantasy with wk25/wk26 lighting. Silhouette differentiation: ranger uses fence/canvas vs warrior wall-paint stack; rogue uses door/window mix and rotated pieces; wizard taller multi-wall stack — likely distinct at default zoom; confirm in Ursina. Optional for Agent 15: if guilds still read too samey at distance, push stronger roof/banner/accent differentiation (do not edit prefabs from Agent 09). Full plan §6 checklist: Jaimie in-game.

style_review.in_game_visual: Ursina session not run here; Jaimie confirms plan section 6 (distinct guild silhouettes, textures vs factor-only, opt-out path).

coordination_notes: Agent 15: stronger accent differentiation only if playtest says guilds blur together.
Agent 10: FPS consult vs WK29 baseline per PM hub.

sprint_meta.notes: Agent 09 implementation: texture-focused polish pass for Inn v2.

wk32_inn_texture_polish_execution.prompt_text: Implement Inn Texture Polish Plan without editing the plan file; complete all todos including screenshots, gates, and Agent 09 log.

what_i_changed.summary: Implemented generated CC0 Inn texture overrides: wood planks, stone blocks, and muted roof shingles. Added cached prefab texture override loading with object-space mapping so Kenney atlas-swatch UVs gain visible texture detail, wired overrides into game renderer and model assembler, added automated screenshot capture flags for model viewer/assembler, and updated the WK30 prefab test layout to include the Inn for side-by-side in-game screenshots. Kept door/window pieces on original materials to preserve readability.

source_reference.note: Textures generated in-repo by tools/generate_inn_texture_overrides.py after online low-poly fantasy reference review; no third-party pixels copied.

validate_assets.note: errors=0; warns=46 existing missing placeholder model warnings

wk32_inn_texture_polish_execution.art_signoff: Pass: Inn roof now has muted shingle texture, stone/curb surfaces use blocky masonry detail, wood-only wall pieces gain plank grain, and doors/windows remain readable. In the prefab-row screenshot the Inn sits closer to the Retro Fantasy stone buildings without losing its Inn silhouette.
coordination_notes: Direct whole-piece texture overrides were viable technically, but Kenney atlas-swatch UVs required object-space texture mapping for visible detail.
Door/window and roof-window pieces intentionally keep original materials to avoid texture damage.

wk32_inn_texture_polish_refinement.prompt_text: Refine Inn texture pass: make stone match Retro Fantasy stone more closely and remove visible original Fantasy Town texture bleed.

what_i_changed.summary: Regenerated the Inn stone texture with warmer tan-grey chunky masonry closer to Retro Fantasy buildings. Expanded texture_override coverage to roof-window and wall-window pieces so original Fantasy Town atlas colors no longer sit over the new roof/wood/stone treatment. Updated prefab override application to clear/apply textures recursively across model child nodes.

validate_assets.note: errors=0; warns=46 existing missing placeholder model warnings

wk32_inn_texture_polish_refinement.art_signoff: Refinement pass accepted: stone is warmer and chunkier like the Retro Fantasy stone family, roof/window wall atlas bleed is removed, and the Inn still preserves its readable door/window silhouette.

wk32_inn_detail_polish_execution.prompt_text: Further the Inn texture work: make door/window/roof-window frames distinct and retune Inn brick scale/value to match the adjacent Retro-style building; iterate with screenshots.

what_i_changed.summary: Refined the generated Inn texture override set with smaller, lighter tan-grey stone blocks and detail-specific door/window/dormer textures. Updated inn_v2 prefab assignments so Fantasy Town door, wall-window, upper wood-window, and roof-window pieces keep the Inn silhouette but receive generated high-contrast frame/glass detail instead of flat broad wall/roof overrides. Iterated through assembler and in-game prefab-row screenshots; rejected a Retro wall-piece swap because it over-darkened the Inn and changed the read too much.

validate_assets.note: errors=0; warns=46 existing missing placeholder model warnings

wk32_inn_detail_polish_execution.art_signoff: Pass: Inn stone now uses smaller lighter tan-grey blocks closer to the adjacent Retro-style masonry, while generated door/window/roof-window detail textures restore dark frame and pale glass reads at close tool view and default in-game row distance. Roof remains muted blue-grey and the Inn still reads as an Inn rather than a generic Retro guild building.
coordination_notes: Kept generated in-repo texture ownership; no third-party pixels copied.
No prefab footprint, anchor, position, rotation, or scale changes.

wk32_inn_door_dormer_refinement.prompt_text: Follow-up polish: center the roof-window read on the dormer outcropping and make the front door visible.

what_i_changed.summary: Moved the visible entrance to the front-center ground panel by swapping that one facade piece to the Retro wall-door mesh, converted the hidden-side door panel back to a window, and enlarged/recentered the generated roof-window detail texture so the dormer window reads inside the roof outcropping. Kept the tuned Inn stone, wood, and roof texture treatment intact.

validate_assets.note: errors=0; warns=46 existing missing placeholder model warnings

wk32_inn_door_dormer_refinement.art_signoff: Pass: front facade now has a visible ground-level door, windows remain readable, and the roof-window detail sits with the dormer outcropping instead of floating off target.
coordination_notes: One Retro Fantasy door piece is now used on the front facade; attribution was updated in inn_v2.json.
No footprint, anchor, position, rotation, or scale changes beyond swapping the facade model kind at the existing front-center slot.

wk32_inn_wrap_window_cleanup.prompt_text: Follow-up polish: make generated stone wrap all around, remove the extra old roof-window mark, and eliminate old window geometry/frame mismatch.

what_i_changed.summary: Converted Inn ground-floor and upper wall window/detail pieces to plain Fantasy Town wall carriers with generated stone/wood/window textures so old window geometry no longer fights the painted frames and the stone treatment wraps around the building. Removed the duplicate rear roof-window piece that was leaving an old window mark on the roof. Added a tiny generated OBJ door overlay with the generated wood texture for a clear entrance without replacing the whole wall panel.

validate_assets.note: errors=0; warns=46 existing missing placeholder model warnings

wk32_inn_wrap_window_cleanup.art_signoff: Pass: generated stone/wood texture treatment now wraps consistently around the Inn, the duplicate roof-window mark is removed, windows no longer show misaligned old mesh detail under the painted frames, and the facade has a readable brown door overlay.
coordination_notes: Added generated OBJ/MTL door overlay under assets/models/buildings and attributed it under kingdomsim_generated_building_textures.
Fixed the tool OBJ loader to use Panda Filename.from_os_specific so Windows paths with spaces load in model viewer/assembler.

wk32_inn_regression_recovery.prompt_text: Human regression report: after adding a door, the Inn roof has a hole, the door makes a hole in the wall, and all windows disappeared. Compare in game, use the model assembler screenshot loop, revise until it looks good.

what_i_changed.summary: Recovered the Inn by restoring real Fantasy Town window meshes for the stone and wood wall windows, reverting the roof-window piece to the normal shingle override instead of the oversized dormer detail texture, and reducing the generated door overlay to one front-facing wood-textured decal on a solid wall panel. This keeps the door readable without using a cut-through door mesh or replacing every window with projection-only painted details.

validate_assets.note: errors=0; warns=46 existing missing placeholder model warnings

wk32_inn_regression_recovery.art_signoff: Pass: regression recovered. The roof no longer reads as punched through, real window geometry is visible again on stone and wood walls, and the visible entrance reads as a brown door panel on solid masonry rather than a black/cut-out hole.
coordination_notes: Root cause was over-converting detailed window meshes to plain wall carriers plus projection-only detail textures; the real window meshes are more reliable for the Inn at current camera distance.
Kept the generated door overlay, but only as a front-facing wood-textured decal so it does not alter the prefab footprint or require a cut-through wall-door mesh.

wk32_inn_roof_window_frame_recovery.prompt_text: Human follow-up: the roof still had a missing middle block and the window frames looked good but were not covering the window holes.

what_i_changed.summary: Restored the second middle roof-window block that had been removed in cleanup, then moved the generated framed-window texture treatment onto solid Fantasy Town wall carriers so the frames/glass cover the whole panel instead of sitting around cut-through window geometry.

validate_assets.note: errors=0; warns=46 existing missing placeholder model warnings

wk32_inn_roof_window_frame_recovery.art_signoff: Pass: the roof middle is filled again and the generated window frames now cover solid panels instead of exposing the old window cutouts.
coordination_notes: The frame artwork was good; the issue was using it on cut-out window meshes. Solid wall carriers are the correct carrier for generated framed-window overrides.

wk32_inn_explicit_window_decals.prompt_text: Human correction: follow instructions and keep screenshot-testing in a loop; windows must be visibly present in the in-game screenshot, not just technically present in the prefab.

what_i_changed.summary: Replaced the fragile projected-window read with explicit generated UV window decal panels on the visible Inn facade. Added a UV texture override mode for generated decal meshes, a generated `inn_window_panel` OBJ/MTL plus texture, and prefab placements that make windows clearly readable in both assembler and in-game row screenshots. The first decal screenshot failed as gray panels; added a UV texture shader and re-shot until windows rendered correctly.

validate_assets.note: errors=0; warns=46 existing missing placeholder model warnings

wk32_inn_explicit_window_decals.art_signoff: Pass: windows are now visible at gameplay distance in the in-game prefab-row screenshot, with blue glass and dark frames on explicit decals over solid wall panels. Roof middle remains filled and the door remains readable.
coordination_notes: Do not accept close tool screenshots alone for small facade details; the in-game row shot is the acceptance gate.
Use UV decal panels when object-space window projection does not prove readable in screenshots.

wk32_r2_visual_followup.prompt_text: After Agents 03 and 15 land: review circled tree fix, denser non-grid grass, added rocks, Inn construction stages. Concise keep/change signoff; no code edits.

what_i_changed.summary: Repo audit and art-direction notes only (no files changed). Confirmed ursina_renderer has _plot_prefab_path(tw,th) → plot_{w}x{h}_v1 for w,h in 1..3, _stage_prefab_path so inn_v2 → inn_v2_build_20_v1 / inn_v2_build_50_v1, _grass_scatter_jitter on grass and doodad placement, and construction staged prefab resolution. R2 prefabs not yet present: missing assets/prefabs/buildings/plot_3x2_v1.json, inn_v2_build_20_v1.json, inn_v2_build_50_v1.json (Inn is 3×2 per inn_v2.json). Until 15 ships those files, the Inn cannot show correct empty plot or partial stages; resolver will fall back per _first_existing. Rocks present with scanner prefix rock: rock.obj, rock_gy_cluster.obj, rock_gy_tall.obj. Tree tiles: only tree_pine.obj matches tree*; circled outlier in Jaimie's screenshot not re-captured here—recommend 15 add/replace a second low-poly pine or tune scale of tree_pine after in-game check.

sprint_meta.notes: WK33 r0: lair static mesh pick, marketplace/blacksmith art briefs, floor_ground_grass albedo sign-off; aligns Phase 1 terrain + Phase 2.2 economy prefabs per master plan

wk33_r0_kickoff.prompt_text: WK33: art direction only — lair Graveyard choice, economy building silhouettes, ground texture approval per wk33 plan and master plan Phase 1–2
references_reviewed: .cursor/plans/master_plan_3d_graphics_v1_5.md (Phase 1 Static Environments; Phase 2 kitbash/texture override bullets)
.cursor/plans/prefab_texture_override_standard.md (skim: flat-kit overrides for Graveyard/Fantasy Town as needed)

lair_mesh.export_target: assets/models/environment/lair_graveyard_crypt_large.glb (or in-place replace assets/models/environment/lair.glb — confirm with Agent 03 wiring)
lair_mesh.rationale: Single static mesh; strong crypt silhouette readable at strategy distance; matches undead / monster-lair fantasy. Lair BUILDING_SIZES span 2x2 (goblin_camp, wolf_den, spider_nest) to 3x3 (skeleton_crypt, bandit_camp) — this asset reads 'building-sized'; Agent 03 should scale/anchor so footprint feels OK for all shared lair types.
alternates_if_too_tall: crypt-a-graveyard.glb or crypt-b-graveyard.glb (smaller A/B crypt variants in same pack)
not_recommended_solo: altar-stone-graveyard (prop scale; weak as sole lair landmark unless kitbashed with walls)

marketplace_silhouette.mood: Open-air market: stall awnings or canopy, low wall / rail, plaza feel — not a sealed fort.
marketplace_silhouette.pieces: Bias Fantasy Town (stalls, cloth, wood trim) + sparse Graveyard/earth props only if it sells 'old kingdom market'; keep piece count low.
marketplace_silhouette.readability: 2x2 footprint per config: silhouette wider than tall; entrance/gap readable toward path.

blacksmith_silhouette.mood: Forge: squat dark mass, **chimney or tall vent stack** breaking the roofline, anvil/forge read (warm stone or override).
blacksmith_silhouette.pieces: Darker stone + metal accents; avoid bright fantasy pastel; use texture_override per prefab standard for flat kit parts.
blacksmith_silhouette.readability: 2x2; chimney/stack makes it distinct from marketplace at a glance.

ground_texture_floor_ground_grass.use: Full-map repeating albedo on base terrain quad (per Agent 03); seamless tile pattern is suitable.
ground_texture_floor_ground_grass.caveat: Texture is **low-value / quite dark** as painted — OK as baseline. After WK33 ~20% scatter lift, if ground still looks muddy or mismatched vs SEEN fog, Agent 03 may apply a **slight** Entity.color multiply on the ground only (e.g. 1.05–1.1) and tune SEEN prop tint separately — do not push grass PNG to 'neon' in isolation.

what_i_changed.summary: Art direction only: chose crypt-large-graveyard for lair; wrote economy prefab briefs; approved floor_ground_grass.png with value note. No game code, assets, or manifest edits.

how_to_test: N/A for Agent 09 this round (direction only). Integrators: run gates after their code changes.

sprint_meta.notes: Hotfix: cleric hero was rendering as a teal circle; update procedural hero sprites so cleric matches the shared pixel-hero style (teal accent).

what_i_changed.summary: Changed HeroSpriteLibrary procedural frames from circle+glyph to a pixel-hero (head/torso/legs) style, matching existing warrior/rogue look at gameplay zoom. Added cleric teal accent (48,186,178) and ensured cleric gets the same animation set. Also added cleric teal to Ursina hero tint + UI portrait color maps, and updated CC0 placeholder generator accent mapping.

coordination_notes: No PNG hero sprites were added; this change standardizes the procedural fallback so new classes (like cleric) do not render as circles.

sprint_meta.notes: Orchestrator tiny sprint: warrior shirt blue→dark red (pygame + Ursina).

what_i_changed.summary: Set HeroSpriteSpec.warrior to (180,45,45) for procedural pygame sprites. Ursina: explicit warrior tint color.white so textured billboards match collab behavior (native sprite colors) and missing-texture fallback is not azure.

orch_r0_impl_then_qa.tint_note: UrsinaEntityRenderCollab.sync_billboard_entity already applies color.white when tex is present and tint_textured is false; warrior branch aligns tint_col with that path and fixes azure-only fallback if tex were absent.

what_i_changed.summary: R0 changed HeroSpriteSpec.warrior to dark red but HeroSpriteLibrary and TerrainTextureBridge cached frames/GPU textures under keys that did not include the palette version, so Ursina could keep serving pre-R0 blue procedural pixels. R1: include hash(HeroSpriteSpec(size)) in HeroSpriteLibrary.clips_for cache keys; append same spec token to Ursina _unit_anim_surface texture cache keys; include spec hash in ursina_texture_bridge.get_hero_idle_texture keys. COLOR_HERO default tint set from azure to HeroSpriteSpec.warrior RGB for missing-texture / unknown-class fallback paths.

orch_r1_bugfix_warrior_red_visual.root_cause_vs_r0: R0 updated procedural pixels but cache keys stayed identical; Ursina billboard path reused stale AnimationClip surfaces and TerrainTextureBridge GPU uploads. Textured billboards still multiply with white (sync_billboard_entity); stale GPU data was the Ursina-blue failure mode.
follow_ups: Agent 11: orchestrator qa_smoke + log fields; Jaimie Ursina + pygame visual gate.

what_i_changed: No edits under game/graphics/ this round. Read Agent 03 canonical log at sprints.orch_tiny_warrior_red.rounds.orch_r3_canonical_log_then_live_blue_diagnosis in agent_03_TechnicalDirector_Architecture.json: live default Ursina path is correct (clips_for → PNG load → TerrainTextureBridge → sync_billboard_entity with white tint); blue warrior shirts are pixel content from assets/sprites/heroes/warrior/*/frame_*.png (shirt probe (70,120,255)), not procedural HeroSpriteSpec / cache-key bugs.
Per assignment: procedural/atlas/clips plumbing was not reassigned to art code — 03 closed the plumbing diagnosis; therefore no hero_sprites.py or bridge changes.

sprint_meta.notes: WK47 R1 baseline atlas: single 2048² RGBA texture packing all hero/enemy/worker frames for instanced shader sampling.

wk47_r1_baseline_atlas.what_i_changed: Added game/graphics/unit_atlas.py: UnitAtlasBuilder singleton packing heroes (5 classes), enemies (6 types), and workers (3 types) via HeroSpriteLibrary / EnemySpriteLibrary / WorkerSpriteLibrary clips_for(size=32); row-based shelf layout into 2048×2048 SRCALPHA surface; normalized UV map keyed by (unit_type, class_key, action, frame_index); lookup_uv fallback to first-cell UV; frame_count for debug.

commands_run.command: python -c "import pygame; pygame.init(); pygame.display.set_mode((1,1)); from game.graphics.unit_atlas import UnitAtlasBuilder; a = UnitAtlasBuilder.get(); print(f'Atlas: {a.atlas_surface.get_size()}, Frames: {a.frame_count}'); uv = a.lookup_uv('hero', 'warrior', 'idle', 0); print(f'Warrior idle 0 UV: {uv}'); assert uv[2] > 0; print('PASS')"

recommended_next_actions: Agent 03 (Wave 2): bind atlas Surface to shared Ursina texture and sample UVs from UnitAtlasBuilder in instanced unit draw path.
